data.title: 'A Fork in the Road,' Says Investor About Micron Stock
data.description:
It has been a whopping ride northbound for Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), as the company has taken off over the past few months. All told, MU has seen its share price catapult by more than 230% during the last half year.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The rising share price is more than just unfounded exuberance. The company's fiscal Q1 2026 revenue of $13.64 billion was a significant sequential jump from the $11.32 billion it reported in fiscal Q4 2025 (and an even bigger increase from the $8.71 billion it delivered in fiscal Q1 2025).

Moreover, Micron's operating cash flow of $8.41 billion wasn't even in the same ballpark as the $3.24 billion it reported in fiscal Q1 2025.

So, with all the great news, why isn't an investment in MU an open-and-shut case? That would be the fears that Micron's experience will be cyclical in nature, and that the seemingly limitless growth of some of the other names from the AI space won't be in the company's future.

Investor Keith Speights is throwing himself into this weighty debate, posing the question that inquiring minds want to know.

"Will Micron be the next Intel? Or will it be the next Nvidia? My answer is neither. Micron will be the next Micron," states the 5-star investor.

Speights acknowledges that no company can escape the law of supply and demand, and Micron is no exception. And Micron, he emphasizes, is a cyclical stock.

However, Speights also points out that this time could be different, as Micron's technology is "invaluable" (in the words of Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, no less) to many of the AI advances taking place.

"I suspect that the demand for AI (and, therefore, the demand for Micron's HBM) will continue for much longer than the memory chip cycles over the last few decades," adds Speights.

That gives the investor enough confidence to predict that MU will be taking the more lucrative fork in the road.

"If I'm right, Micron could be a lot more like Nvidia than Intel going forward," concludes Speights. (To watch Keith Speights' track record, click here)

Wall Street is also banking on continued growth, with a caveat. MU's 26 Buys and 2 Holds combine to give it a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, it seems the gains have shot up faster than some of the bulls had anticipated, as its 12-month average price target implies minimal downside. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Jobs Could be the Latest Victim of AI, Cautions Mohamed El-Erian
data.description:
January's jobs report was a welcome sight for many, showing 130,000 nonfarm payroll additions and the unemployment rate falling to 4.3% from 4.4%. However, cracks are starting to emerge underneath the surface, according to Allianz Chief Economic Adviser Mohamed El-Erian.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

El-Erian points out that the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) revised nonfarm payrolls for December and November lower by a combined 99,000 jobs. In addition, the BLS revised total payroll gains in 2025 to 181,000 from 584,000, marking the lowest level of hiring outside of a recession since 2003.

U.S. Jobs Decouple from GDP

At the same time, the U.S. economy continues to grow at a healthy pace, demonstrating a decoupling of job growth from gross domestic product (GDP).

"There are also reasons to believe that this period of decoupling of employment from growth may prove more persistent and more consequential," said El-Erian. These reasons include the onset of AI and a widespread affordability crisis. With strong economic growth and a weak labor market, wealth inequality could widen even further, raising the risk of social and economic tensions.

data.title: Prepare for the 'Dark Side of AI,' Warns Wall Street
data.description:
The boom in AI has created several side effects that have caught many investors by surprise. For example, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) is down by 19% year-to-date amid concerns that AI will intensify competition and lower sales within the industry. Last week, the freight industry was the latest to be hit after Algorhythm Holdings (RIME) announced that its AI-driven SemiCab platform had scaled freight volumes by 300% to 400% without increasing headcount.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"That's the dark side of AI," Innovator Capital Management Chief Investment Strategist Tim Urbanowicz told Yahoo Finance. "We need to pay attention to that because I do think there's going to be other industries that are disrupted, and this is certainly a threat."

AI Uncertainty Pressures Stocks, Although Winners Could Emerge

AI has raised uncertainty about future cash flows, lowering stock valuations in the process. At the same time, companies that are able to use AI as a tool to innovate their core businesses could "benefit, especially those in the financials and health care sectors," said UBS Global Wealth Management CIO Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi.

While several sectors have experienced poor performance in 2026, the S&P 500 (SPX) is down by less than 1%, demonstrating strong market breadth. Urbanowicz expects the benchmark index to end the year at 7,600, citing tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill and a favorable regulatory environment.

data.title: The $9 Million SafeMoon Betrayal Ends with Braden John Karony Behind Bars for 8 Years
data.description:
The SafeMoon saga has officially reached its final chapter in a Brooklyn federal court. U.S. District Judge Eric Komitee sentenced the company's former CEO, Braden John Karony, to eight years and four months in federal prison. This ruling follows a May 2025 conviction where a jury found Karony guilty of conspiracy to commit securities fraud, wire fraud, and money laundering. While SafeMoon once promised to take its holders "Safely to the Moon," prosecutors successfully proved that the project was actually a vehicle for Karony to drain millions from the pockets of hard-working investors.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Karony Exploits the Liquidity Pool

The core of the fraud centered on SafeMoon's unique transaction tax. Every time someone traded the token, a 10% fee was collected; half was supposed to be redistributed to holders, and the other half was meant to be locked in a liquidity pool to stabilize the price. Karony assured the "SafeMoon Army" that these funds were inaccessible to developers and would only be used for business growth. In reality, prosecutors revealed that Karony and his partners kept secret backdoor access to these pools. Instead of keeping the market stable, Karony moved over $9 million in cryptocurrency into private wallets to bankroll his personal desires.

Greed Drives Luxury Purchases

The stolen funds fueled a lifestyle that most investors could only dream of. During the trial, the government detailed how Karony treated investor money like a personal ATM to buy high-end assets. The proceeds from the liquidity pool paid for a $2.2 million mansion in Utah, along with additional homes in Kansas. The driveway of his sprawling real estate featured a fleet of expensive vehicles, including a $277,000 Audi R8 (VWAGY), a Tesla (TSLA), and custom trucks like a Ford F-550 (F) and a Jeep Gladiator. United States Attorney Joseph Nocella, Jr. noted that Karony lied to investors from all walks of life, including veterans and hard-working Americans, just to maintain this "lavish lifestyle."

Law Enforcement Traces the Scheme

The downfall of the SafeMoon executive team happened because of a combination of blockchain forensics and traditional investigative work. Agents from the IRS and the FBI followed the digital breadcrumbs through complex transaction routing and hidden exchange accounts. While Karony tried to mask the movement of the stolen tokens, law enforcement eventually exposed the scheme, leading to the forfeiture of $7.5 million and two residential properties. One co-conspirator, Thomas Smith, has already pleaded guilty and is awaiting his own fate, while the project's creator, Kyle Nagy, remains a fugitive.

Key Takeaway

the SafeMoon story is a classic case of a rug pull hidden behind complicated tech jargon. Braden Karony used the excitement of a meme coin and the complexity of a liquidity pool to trick people into thinking their money was locked and safe. In reality, he was just waiting for the right moment to empty the vault and buy a custom Audi.

Investors can track the prices of well-established cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Micron (MU) Sounds Alarm on AI Memory Shortage as DRAM Prices Spike Into 2027
data.description:
A growing shortage of DRAM and NAND chips is putting pressure on tech firms around the world. From car makers to phone brands, many firms now say memory supply is tight, and costs are rising. As a result, profit margins may narrow, and some product plans may shift in 2026.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Micron Technology (MU) has called the current bottleneck "unprecedented." The company says demand tied to AI data centers is taking up a large share of global supply, meaning there are not enough memory chips to go around.

The core issue is the rapid buildout of AI data hubs. Firms such as Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta Platforms (META) are set to spend about $650 billion on AI gear in 2026, up from about $360 billion last year. Much of that spend goes toward servers that use Nvidia (NVDA) AI chips. These systems require large amounts of memory, especially high-bandwidth memory, which draws on the same supply chain as other DRAM products.

Micron Executive Vice President Manish Bhatia said high bandwidth memory for AI chips is "consuming so much of the available capacity across the industry that it's leaving a tremendous shortage for the conventional side."

At the same time, memory prices are moving fast. One type of DRAM rose 75% from December to January, according to Bernstein. When prices jump at that pace, buyers often rush to secure stock, which can add more strain.

Pressure on Margins and Product Plans

The impact is already visible. Apple (AAPL) said higher memory costs will weigh on iPhone margins. The firm guided to gross margins of 48% to 49% for the March quarter. That range reflects rising input costs tied to memory chips.

Meanwhile, Tesla (TSLA) is also feeling the squeeze. Elon Musk said during the firm's earnings call, "We've got two choices: hit the chip wall or make a fab." His comment suggests that securing a long-term memory supply may require greater control over production.

The ripple effect extends further. Nvidia is said to be delaying new gaming GPUs as it directs limited memory to AI chips. Sony Group (SONY) is weighing whether to delay its next PlayStation console, while Nintendo (NTDOY) may review pricing for the Switch 2. In autos, analysts report signs of early stockpiling as car makers seek to avoid future gaps in supply.

Moreover, relief may not come soon. Building new memory plants takes years and costs billions of dollars. Synopsys (SNPS) CEO Sassine Ghazi told CNBC that the shortage is likely to persist through 2026 and 2027. Lenovo Group (LNVGY) CEO Yang Yuanqing also said the crunch will last at least through the rest of this year.

Memory makers such as Micron, Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), and SK Hynix (HK:7709) may benefit from firmer pricing. At the same time, hardware firms that rely on DRAM and NAND could face margin pressure in the near term.

Using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we've assembled all the tickers mentioned in the piece to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader memory chip industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: 'More Doom and Gloom,' Says Investor About Robinhood Stock
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) has made its name by providing a platform for everyone to have equal access to the financial markets, and "not just the wealthy." The company seems to have struck a chord, and it recently finished 2025 with 28.4 million investment accounts (up 2.2 million from the previous year).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A glance at its 2025 financial figures reflects both top- and bottom-line growth as well. The company achieved record revenues of $4.5 billion last year, while its Diluted EPS of $2.05 was also a new high.

However, the past few months haven't been great for investors in HOOD, as the company's share price has been almost cut in half since early October.

Investor Anthony Di Pizio points out that the overall mood in the market has a tendency to impact Robinhood's prospects. That makes him worried about what comes next.           

"Robinhood's investing platform is very popular with young, often first-time investors who trade very actively when the financial markets are strong, but who also have a tendency to retreat when gloom sets in," explains the investor.

The investor draws a parallel between 2021 and 2025, arguing that rallies in both years were driven by "speculative investing activity." Much of this was focused in the cryptocurrency market, and Di Pizio notes that Robinhood's crypto revenue soared in 2021 as investors rushed to purchase tokens such as Dogecoin.

Once the crypto rally ended the following year, Robinhood's crypto transaction revenues took a nosedive. The investor believes a similar dynamic is transpiring.

"I want to focus on Robinhood's cryptocurrency business, which I believe is the main source of the recent plunge in its stock," adds Di Pizio. "I think a repeat of 2022 might be on the table, so Robinhood's crypto struggles could get worse."

That makes the investor fairly convinced that HOOD, which is currently trading above its historic price-to-sales ratio, is primed "for even more downside from here." (To watch Anthony Di Pizio's track record, click here)

Wall Street is singing a very different tune, however. With 14 Buys and 2 Holds, HOOD enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $135.46 points to gains approaching 80%. (See HOOD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross
